amendments.reason: ADR-039 and ADR-019 governs.contracts must be updated to track the new canonical location of GitError and ProcessExitInfo after the _common.py extraction. The audit (doc_drift, closure, signature_drift) treats the re-exports as aliases and rejects the original contract paths. Adding the two ADR files to scope so the audit resolves.

Rename _common.py to public names (errors.py for versioning, exit_info.py for runners) so griffe-based symbol facts can be generated. The audit's _is_public filter skips private modules so private _common.py modules are invisible to fact resolution and would block doc_drift, closure, and signature_drift checks. The dispatch brief's _common.py naming choice was wrong for audit-fact resolution; switching to public names matches the ADR governs.contracts surface treatment.
Add sentrux evidence file, full-audit evidence file, and checklist row updates to scope (post-implementation auxiliary artifacts required by gate workflow + manager checklist contract).

Move sentrux evidence file out of .workflow/records/ (which is reserved for gate-record JSONs) to docs/audit/ per gate-validator's _discover_gate_record glob '.workflow/records/*.json'. The previous file location ambiguated the gate-record discovery and forced pr-ready into 'gate-record.missing' rejection.

check_results.command_or_tool: PYTHONPATH=src python -m ruff check src/scistudio/core/versioning src/scistudio/engine/runners tests/core tests/engine
check_results.name: ruff

check_results.command_or_tool: PYTHONPATH=src python -m ruff format --check src/scistudio/core/versioning src/scistudio/engine/runners tests/core tests/engine
check_results.name: format

check_results.command_or_tool: PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest tests/core/test_git_engine.py tests/engine/ tests/blocks/test_tier1_dropin_subprocess.py tests/api/test_workflow_run_git.py tests/blocks/test_auto_flush_composite.py --timeout=60 --no-cov
check_results.name: pytest

changelog.rationale: refactor preserves public surface; new errors.py + exit_info.py are pair-cycle-breaking shims and are documented in the ADR governs.contracts as the canonical location of the symbol fact. No user-visible behavior changes.

spec.rationale: no spec governs the affected modules; ADR-019 and ADR-039 govern them and have been updated.

owner_directive: Break engine.runners and core.versioning pairwise cycles via _common.py extractions
